rationale: Keep: the cited paper directly studies recommendation in a social-plus-product dual network (YouTube/Last.fm) and shows how user-generated social links change exploration outcomes, which aligns with the citing paper’s core constructs (content-based vs social filtering, user behavior changes, concentration effects). The citing note says it extends prior recommender research in e-commerce settings, and this 2012 JMR article appears to be a likely foundational/mechanism prior rather than an incidental mention.
